delivery.raw: 主に対面（全開講回数の過半数）、一部オンライン

rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary: This course explores how people learn languages and how language classes are conducted in different educational contexts. Students will consider how language and learning are connected across various learning environments with a focus on the English language.
aims: The main aim of this course is to learn the basic principles of second language acquisition and language education approaches. After learning these principles, students will be able to apply that knowledge to understand and explain their own language learning experiences.
goals: Students will gain a basic understanding of Second Language Acquisition theories
Students will become more familiar with technical terminology related to Second Language Acquisition as well as language learning and teaching terms
Attain basic knowledge of English education, especially in Japan.
Students will be able to make connections between Second Language Acquisition theory and their language learning experiences.
Students will be able to make connections between language teaching and learning approaches with their educational experiences

plan.text: Unit 1 - Introduction to the course. Begin the first topic: What does it mean to "know" a language?
Unit 1 -The differences between First Language Acquisition (FLA) and Second Language Acquisition SLA
Unit 2 -Communicative competence. What should language learners be able to do?
Unit 2 - Why listening is difficult for language learners. Cognitive and affective factors.
Unit 2 - Speaking challenges with fluency, accuracy and anxiety.
Unit 2 - The role of output in language development
Midterm exam - The exam will cover material from Unit 1 and 2.
Unit 3 - What is a Task? Definitions and Pedagogical Rationale
Unit 3 - Focus on Form, Focus on Forms and Focus on Meaning + Key Techniques
Unit 3 - Practical Applications of Focus on Form in the Classroom
Unit 3 - Error Correction and Feedback in SLA
Unit 4 - Course Review and Reflective Discussion
Final exam - The exam will cover material from Unit 3 and 4

textbooks: There is no textbook for this class. The instructor will provide all handouts via Teams.
prereq: Students who enrol in this class should have a strong interest in the fields of language education and Second Language Acquisition (SLA). The class will be taught in English, so students should be willing to listen to lectures, participate in class activities, and read handouts in English. Please note that for students who have already taken Theories of Second Language Acquisition, many of the topics covered will be a review.
prep: Students will need to read and review class materials to prepare for class lectures and participate actively in any class pair or group work.
teachers: ◎ デイナ リングリー

extra.text: ①令和５年度以前入学生対象科目名:第二言語習得基礎論 ②本授業の内容は、文化審議会国語分科会『日本語教育人材の養成・研修の在り方について（報告）改訂版』に示された「必須の教育内容」50項目のうち、(15)言語学習、(16)習得過程、(17)学習ストラテジー、(19)日本語の学習・教育の情意的側面、(29)中間言語分析、(34)コミュニケーション教育に対応するものです。
